steps.title: Complete both squares
steps.content:
Group the $x$ terms and $y$ terms, then complete the square for each group:
$$
x^2+x=(x+\frac{1}{2})^2-\frac{1}{4}
$$
$$
y^2+y=(y+\frac{1}{2})^2-\frac{1}{4}
$$

steps.title: Write circle form
steps.content:
Substitute those forms into the equation:
$$
(x+\frac{1}{2})^2+(y+\frac{1}{2})^2-\frac{1}{2}=\frac{241}{2}
$$
Move the constant to the other side:
$$
(x+\frac{1}{2})^2+(y+\frac{1}{2})^2=121
$$

steps.title: Read the radius
steps.content: In standard circle form, the right side is $r^2$: $$ r^2=121 $$ $$ r=11 $$ The answer is 11.
